attack_summary: A host-compromise and data-exfiltration attack on UbuntuComputer, centered on a malicious splunkd-backed operator workflow that attempted to persist, stage files locally, and exfiltrate data while abusing network tooling and outbound traffic.

mitigations.name: Terminate/disable malicious splunkd operator workflow
mitigations.brief_description: Killed the live splunkd process on UbuntuComputer and made the malicious /usr/src/app/splunkd binary non-executable to stop the active attacker workflow.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: FortiGate outbound egress allowlisting/deny policy
mitigations.brief_description: Attempted to restrict Ubuntu outbound traffic to a narrow allowlist and deny general HTTPS exfiltration, but the policy was inconsistently applied and did not stop the attack.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Restrict permissions on /usr/src/app
mitigations.brief_description: Hardened directory permissions on /usr/src/app and related staging paths to block local collection/archiving, but the exfiltration workflow still succeeded.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Integrity monitoring for splunkd and working directory
mitigations.brief_description: Deployed a baseline-and-watch script with cron to detect restoration of execute permission or binary replacement, but it only improved detection and did not prevent the attack.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Ubuntu local access-control tightening for sensitive directories
mitigations.brief_description: Applied broader UNIX permission changes on staging and image/data directories to block read/archive access, but the attacker still completed the workflow.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Enforce splunkd non-executable state
mitigations.brief_description: Set splunkd permissions to 000 and attempted immutability to keep the malicious binary from running again, but it did not reduce attack success.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Disable attacker tooling (hping3, nmap)
mitigations.brief_description: Removed execute permissions from common reconnaissance/abuse tools on UbuntuComputer, but this did not disrupt the exfiltration attack.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: FortiGate logging/alerting for outbound exfiltration
mitigations.brief_description: Added logging/alerting for outbound traffic from UbuntuComputer to improve visibility, but it did not stop the attacker’s behavior.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

key_observations: The only clearly effective action was directly stopping the active malicious process and disabling the splunkd binary.
Host-hardening and permission changes were generally connectivity-safe but did not reduce the attacker’s success rate.
Network filtering was attempted twice in different forms, but policy enforcement was inconsistent and ineffective against exfiltration.
Monitoring and logging controls improved detection/visibility but were not preventive mitigations.
Across later attempts, the attacker’s success rate repeatedly remained 1.0, indicating the core exfiltration path stayed intact.
overall_conclusion: The campaign was a host-based malicious workflow on UbuntuComputer performing exfiltration. Most mitigations were operationally safe but ineffective; only direct process termination and disabling the malicious splunkd binary produced a successful stop. Defensive measures that relied on permissions, monitoring, or incomplete firewall policy changes did not materially impair the attack.
